title: COVID-19: What News Can You Believe？ [Aesthetics Mastery Show]

I hope that ever when out there who doesn't quite grasp this does grasp it as soon as possible I know there are still lots of people who believe in conspiracy theories and there are lots of people who are you know basically saying it'll be fine it's all blown out of proportion and stuff but it's just you can literally watch what's happening in Italy and see it coming our way so but on the flip side though I my personally believe that we emotionally we've got a choice either do or die and I'm I mean don't get me wrong because I business on Saturday that was not loving life but on Sunday I'm like literally like I've got a choice you know I've got a feed my children so now what's the next step what's next that's where I'm at yeah well it absolutely there isn't much point looking down and thinking I'm feeling crap about it like you we have to you just have to ask yourself what can I do so I was thinking about this actually in terms of mental health and as my work as a GP I discovered fairly very quickly when you talk to people about mental health

problems that one of the biggest reasons why they and they feel depressed or anxious is because of a focus on things they cannot control so what does that mean practically it means take control of the things that you do have control over because that will give you the mental strength to take on bigger and bigger challenges and so that's kind of what we're doing first and what tell us about will the first thing you did on Sunday morning yeah and it came I wasn't particularly thinking oh let's do a mindset tool or the thing I just it just flowed out me it one piece of paper and I wrote down literally get dressed put makeup on have shower you know do exercise and you know those things to be able to do those things and literally tick them off mate meant the world to me make bed you know it honestly it makes the world of difference if you can even just stand up like stand up from the sofa or if you're feeling rubbish stand up ideally go to power folks you know drink a glass of water these things

make such a difference is that but will we do so much more our minds they won't we weeks months yeah so we talk about closing our business a bit more detail so on Friday night Boris Johnson announced that the Pops and gyms were to close and when you dug into the guidances also said sparse Wellness Center's massage parlors so for anyone who's still wondering whether you should be open you know of course that's your own personal decision to make but we felt that basically meant us without saying out loud and then on Saturday we had a directors meeting in the morning via zoom and then we yeah we let's talk a little bit about the process of going into closing business because I'm seeing this internal battle going on with people all the time and it was you know it's no small thing and I think we should all remember that people closing their businesses down I've seen people say oh they're putting profit before people most of us are not after profit anymore if this is survival like it's everything that you might have

worked for the last 1012 years in our case that might completely disappear and you are trying to try to optimize your decisions to maximize your chance of coming out the other end with anything at all so I do think we need to be kind to those businesses that haven't shut yet at the same time we should encourage them to shut so there was at least one discussion I had on Facebook with someone where I believe I was I helped them make the decision because they were saying we're gonna wipe down our services and all that stuff and I just basically said look I know this is a tough decision then you're trying your best to get through this you're probably picturing the impact on your family of not having an income that this is the right time to close there are a couple of good reasons why even selfishly you might want to close basically the longer people don't take the advice the longer this is going to go on for and secondly the there is also the chance that this could get very bad in your neighborhood and people will remember those businesses who ignore the advice and you might find yourself on the wrong end of some very bad PR so I do

think you should get ahead of it closed down decisively the more people who do this as possible the more you encourage other people to do this who are being asked to close down the short of this might be because every single person who catches this illness is passing on to other and if you can stop that cycle by closing a business evil for two or three people the impact a few weeks down the line can be hundreds of people who are less who aren't sick so let's be kind to those people but let's also you know go on a bit of a social media drive to actually encourage them to Shutts empathizing that it's difficult but it's the right thing to do because I no one knows the position that anyone else is in so for example we have 33 star and so we also have - of course - you know they're not in mind so it's incredibly hard yeah and many of our staff were like let's you know let's keep going we'll try it we're trying our best you know let's go and of course they'll because it's also worth remembering that if you're not in a Health System you don't actually have anything else to do like I I'm in a

lucky position in some ways and I could go back to general practice I can earn a living and other people are not in that position so we do need to understand that this is from the point of view of aesthetics practitioners if you're working partly NHS in part not in some ways this is going to impact you less even if your business is lost than those people who are facing losing their job and be left in the middle of a recession because that's almost certainly what we're going to get at the end of this and trying to look for work so it's a big thing and we need to bring kindness yeah while also encouraging people to make the right decision so let's segue then onto that topic of going back in it yes because I've been obsessively trucking this tidal wave of emotion because I'm so into mindset over every single hour this last week particularly and it felt like at the beginning of last week so about a week ago people were oh god I don't know whether I want to go back seeing that chairs new baby people will be bullied

there or you know they had a bad circumstance in their life that they didn't want to be on the frontline in the past but then towards the end of the week mid towards in the week people were really thinking you know they have up that tug on their heart so I should go back and help so tell us about your journey well yeah I mean initially you're focused on trying to keep what you have together and as its closing down so it's you know it's painful I felt really awful telling staff you know I had that was part of me that was hoping we could somehow like keep something together but there's nothing left to do so you know we are going to have to basically send people home which without any plan for how they come back which is really upsetting so I was really concerned at this of having to tell people I know depend on the income that that's that it's we just don't have an answer so that was all false in my mind was on that but then obviously the news on Italy coming through and particularly in the esthetics world is there's a sense

of people returning so yeah I kind I found it really emotional seeing my colleagues go back and because they're required and you know a lot of them going into dangerous situations and together so will I be I'm trying to get hold of better PP mask in general practice then it feels like the right thing to do when the country's in a crisis so no I don't think our generation has ever had anything quite like it and it does feel like a wartime spirit you know we've particularly for health care professionals who essentially we are a tribe and it is different being a health care professional because you're and yo you knighted amongst the worst things that happen in people's lives like you are the foots the foot soldiers who you try and prevent people from dying unnecessarily so and it's an honor to be part of it and I'm really pleased I can go back and I've had that confirmation yesterday that I'll be I will be back working on the front line and so it feels great to contribute that at the same time it's

scary you know we've got two kids I don't want to be one of the unlucky younger people who react badly so I'm going to try and take as many precautions as I can we all have that battle of like personal theater sirs and looking after ourselves and our family versus contributing to the community but what an amazing thing to see certainly practitioners go back and there are many of them who have shut up and volunteer their services pretty much the same day that's what I did and I think it's a beautiful thing and everyone who's doing this should be really proud I think it's a it's amazing that we get to of watching us all the Avengers sign come up a little bit like that Avengers are 17 because we're all in the background doing our aesthetic stuff and but we've now really grounded in something which is fundamental which is life and death you know I think beauty is a really beautiful thing like I think bringing beauty to the world making feel making people feel better is so valuable but there is a deeper human need which

is just to survive and that we can go back to contributing to that is a good thing so let's briefly turn to the third topic which is around fake news Corona fake news and from my perspective the news is something that of course helps us but can totally hinder us even setting aside fake news it can be just very hard on the emotions worried what with news and well I certainly learned a lesson on fake news because I fell for a little rumor that was being spread as essentially a photograph of one of our colleagues who's who is actually very ill in hospital with corona virus Kareena she's a nurse and someone posted on to the local support group in that part of the country that she had died and then quick very quickly this has spread around our community because we're all devastated to hear that and but it turns out that's not true and she's actually hopefully but it's very hard to find out what the real facts are but hopefully and more than on the positive side although we don't know for

sure and but this gets spread around and then people who know these people get that horrible shock if I mind my members died my colleague has died and it's not true and I don't know why these things happen I don't know if it's just simply a rumor or if it's a if someone who's like oh I wonder if I go viral and spread some news I don't know I really hope it's not people being you know because a little bit like what happened with me I got it through a colleague support network so some and I a group that I trusted shared it I almost assumed that they knew her or she was you know maybe they work with her because everyone was reacting as if it was true so I shared it and then thankfully the rumors weren't true so and but there's there are actually there are a few other things going on as well which is which I've noticed which is the certainly one Daily Mail article where enough we were going over again this morning and I hope some of the screen shot at it because I think it may have been edited but they it's supposedly from this anonymous doctor who's written writing about his experiences and you know it's very emotional they're saying he's talking about how you can see the fear

in the patient's eyes and all his colleagues are panicking and it's not actually how most doctors talk about these situations I mean the honest truth is you it's actually quite amazing how people in this situation deal with emergencies I mean it's very stressful no one likes it but you what you'll see is a team usually pulling together efficiently doing what needs to be done you see them looking stressed but it's not they don't you will very seldom see if team panicking it does happen but I don't feel like this is necessarily yes I think that you know maybe in two weeks time will be an open a whole worse situation hopefully not but it just met everyone reading it who's in the nose like this doesn't sound like how a doctor would describe in fact there was a component part of it where he's talking about his pager going off which is not really what doctors tend to call him these days you believe or and then he talks about the respiratory arrest and having to do cardiopulmonary resuscitation and it's it doesn't quite match like yes

respiratory arrest do lead to cardiac arrest but if there are just lots of things are you thinking this does not sound like an experienced doctor writing this sounds like someone trying to imitate an experienced doctor now I really hope I hope there isn't the doctor behind that what now that I've said that but what I want people to be cautious of because I'm having burned of is people are trying to get attention that people are trying to get and attention particularly you know if you think about how the Daily Mail works most of their revenue comes through the number of clicks they get and the number of people who see the adverts on those pages so that's how they fund themselves so they absolutely do want to write things that get your attention and sometimes those things are valid news like things that get your attention because they're important and sometimes they are exaggerations in order to get and to get your attention and so that they get paid so they're more advertising sales people than they are and journalists and many of these online papers so we all know in this day and

age have an audience even it's just our friends and we all have the ability to trumpet things that for example those are something went around various what's up screwed about 24 hours ago which was about how petrol pumps are very bad for conducts in the creative arts when I saw that I mean maybe they I have no evidence either way but I think I did wonder like is that true or is it scare mongering so where's the line and what could we do as you know as people who have a responsibility for truth passing on what can we do to only pass on the correct truth well the first thing is have a little gap between when you see something when you acceptor is true like that's what I've learned from the situation is and it's often the hardest ones where it's presented through a friend who you really trust who tells you the news you know they're not lying they believe it true and that makes you less likely to question it yourself so there is I've been burned out through that mistake I'm gonna

remember it and try it much harder to dig deeper before I share something the petrol pump thing is a great example of I'm fairly certain that's nonsense because what's the difference in a petrol pump and a shopping trolley you might use them on the same day you're holding them where should we go to you to get the facts well facts is a strong word I think it's a I think it's really hard to get you can say what authorities think are the facts but to actually establish the facts it you know we've got a long way to go before we know that but this is where it gets tricky is because um you know we do need the information but you just got to try and try not to share everything that's that comes you away and try and interrogate them a little bit and the authorities are important you know I do think like the World Health Organization that's an authority but they also have an agenda one of their agendas is to get people to take this seriously so I don't think for example the report and the 3.4% death rate and was caveated with the likelihood that it's going to be much

less than that I think the point of releasing that data was to get people to wake up yeah what about the option I'm not watching any news just to keep our sanity I don't think that's a good strategy when things are changing so fast I think we need to know what's going on but at the same time you might want to limit it for your sanity so maybe it's an hourly update and in the middle of the day because I don't think it's a good way to start your day with low bad news and it's probably not a good way to end the day you go from a mental health point of view it's a good event trying to digest the news so maybe it's a lunchtime thing when you catch up and then you try not to look at it again if it's stressing you out and but I mean this is affecting everyone in ways that they're not yet able to fully process and I don't recommend hiding from it and I think we should know what's going on but then also take measures to protect your mental health I'm gonna be brutally honest I watching the news every hour no way do you mean swats the middle today yeah I'm saying I mean it depends how you were affected by it I certainly on Sunday went down a rabbit

hole and you know especially the clips of Italian hospitals and got me really stressed out and then I was like okay I need to not do anything and I went and lay on the driveway and meditated and I thought what's better and they said because I'm they came and jumped on my chest and humanity and then helium's because was cold touch up the floor so he just lay it lay on top of me I mean a moment so I felt a bit better and after that there's a difference between the data and the anecdotes in terms of how you respond to them and this is one of things I find difficult in terms of facts because anecdotes from the medical perspective we know that anecdotes aren't data you can't tell what's going on from the anecdote but the problem is you also emotionally resonate with facts so that this is well known also in terms of a how people give to charity if you say there are million people starving you get less money than if you say this little boy in Somalia is starving people want to help the individual and it's more real we understand people we don't understand numbers on an emotional level

so when they said that 800 people have died in Italy that's that that's a really terrifying number for some reason me seeing the i teed itu department with people rushing about and people on ventilators that hit me more about the reality because you can see it's a and that wasn't even an IT department actually that was just a normal ward that looked like an IT department that reality wakes me up on an emotional level to what the situation really is now obviously it's not complete reality because maybe who knows you know maybe next door they're all having cups of tea and they're showing out you know you're not you're never getting the full component of it that the anecdotes the stories individual stories I think they work on an emotional level which the facts don't and there's something about it about understanding it on that level too as well as understanding the facts I think it's as ever in life its balance it's about understanding a reality but being in a position to be able to do something about that reality because I

think we were all on or close to an edge and many more news reports might just tip it over the edge where we are basically not functioning and we are having mental breakdowns and cetera so for me it's about seeing the facts seeing the anecdotes understanding them taking them in but not allowing it to penetrators useless so and some pretty dark things that we're facing at the moment but I also want to say that and spring always comes from these things it doesn't matter how dark things get and we are going to come out the other side of it and life will return to something like what we remember it to be with some lessons learned I actually think we'll probably all come out of it slightly better people maybe a little bit more well-rounded we would have learned something we'll appreciate things we didn't appreciate before and there's gonna be a lot of good things that come out of this as well as all the bad stuff we're going through right now right guys we'll be seeing a lot more of you because we've got a camera we've got

thoughts we've got big hearts and we want to share everything that's flowing through us with you guys so watch out for everything that's coming your way and please do let us know in the comments below it is anything more that you'd like us to discuss that would serve you for all of you going back on the frontline thank you very much and you're deeply appreciated and every single one of you whether you're a healthcare assistant or any retired and coming back in and you're all deeply valued and thank you very much [Music]
